mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes A200 Petrol engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned A200 petrol engine costs £3,000–£5,500 supply only depending on which generation you have. The W176 M270 2.0-litre starts around £3,500; the W177 M282 1.3-litre starts around £3,000. Reconditioned OM133.980 (M282) engines are available from approximately £2,390. Dealer engine replacements typically cost £4,000–£7,000. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,600 depending on your location and workshop.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes A200 Petrol engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which generation you have. The W176's M270 2.0-litre is a proven, reliable engine that's well-regarded by owners. The W177's M282 1.3-litre is a modern, efficient engine but has known issues: turbocharger oil feed line leaks (recall), ticking noises, and carbon buildup on intake valves. Both are good engines when properly maintained.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes A200 Petrol?

acceptedAnswer.text: W176 M270: Timing chain wear, high-pressure fuel pump issues, water pump failure, carbon buildup. W177 M282: Turbocharger oil feed line leaks (recall), ticking/rattling noises at idle, carbon buildup on intake valves, injector issues, and occasional timing chain wear. The specific issues depend on which generation you have.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes A200 Petrol engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and which generation. Against a well-kept £18,000 W177 A200, a £4,000 recon is 22% — worth it. Against a tired £6,000 W176 example, the same recon approaches our 70% Mercedes threshold — get a scrap valuation first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes A200 Petrol engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. W176 (2013–2018): M270DE20LA. W177 (2018–present): M282DE14AL. The chassis code is the easiest tell — W176 is the 3rd generation A-Class, W177 is the 4th generation.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the turbo oil leak on the M282, or does it need full engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the turbocharger oil feed line leak is a well-understood repair that may be covered by a recall. Replacement of the oil feed line costs £300–£700. If you notice oil spots or a burning smell, have it inspected immediately — oil leaks can lead to fire risk in some cases. This is a repair, not an engine replacement issue.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes A200 Petrol Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the M270 and M282 petrol engines. Reconditioned OM133.980 (M282) engines are available from approximately £2,390. Dealer engine replacements typically cost £4,000–£7,000. Main dealer new engines can exceed £10,000.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an A200 engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £70–£100/hr (independent) or £120–£180/hr (London/SE dealer).

cta.label: → Compare A200 petrol engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Turbocharger Oil Feed Line Leak — M282 (W177)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the car, burning oil smell from the engine bay, low oil level warning, visible oil residue around the turbocharger area.
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — recall affected multiple model years
problems.repairCost: £300–£700 (oil feed line replacement — recall may cover it)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.recommendation: The M282 engine has a known issue with the turbocharger oil feed line. Mercedes-Benz issued a recall (2020) for A180, A200, B180, and CLA200 models. Engine oil may escape from the line, leak onto the underfloor panelling and from there onto the road. In some cases, oil leaks can lead to fire risk. If you notice oil spots or a burning smell, have the turbocharger oil feed line inspected immediately. Check if the recall has been completed on your vehicle.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil leak guide

problems.title: Engine Ticking / Rattling Noises — M282 (W177)
problems.symptoms: Ticking noise at idle, metallic ticking that gets faster with engine speed, squeaking noise on startup, rough idle.
problems.repairCost: £200–£800 (diagnosis; potential injector or timing chain work)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.replacementCost: £3,000–£7,500 (if timing chain failure leads to engine damage)
problems.recommendation: Many owners report ticking or rattling noises from the M282 engine. Some garages dismiss this as normal for these engines, but persistent ticking can indicate injector issues or timing chain wear. If you notice unusual noises, have the engine inspected. The 1.3-litre M282 uses a timing chain (not a belt).

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: Carbon Buildup on Intake Valves — M282 (W177)
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, hesitation on acceleration, loss of power, increased fuel consumption, engine warning light.
problems.repairCost: £300–£800 (walnut blasting / intake valve cleaning)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.recommendation: Direct injection engines like the M282 are prone to carbon buildup on intake valves. This can cause rough running and loss of performance. Regular use of high-quality fuel and occasional Italian tune-ups can help reduce buildup. If symptoms persist, walnut blasting of the intake valves is the recommended solution.

failureLink.label: Read the full carbon buildup guide

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: A200 Petrol — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Recall may cover it; straightforward replacement
Diagnosis is key — could be minor or serious
Walnut blasting — effective and relatively affordable
Preventative — catches the fault before catastrophic failure
Well-understood repair; cheaper than engine replacement

buyingChecks.title: Buying This A200 Petrol? Check These First
buyIf: * W176 (M270): Full Mercedes service history with evidence of regular oil changes — a proven, reliable engine
* W177 (M282): Turbocharger oil feed line recall completed
* W177 (M282): No unusual ticking or rattling noises at idle
* Any generation: Full service history with regular oil changes using MB-spec oil
avoidIf: * W177 (M282): Oil leaks with no documented recall work
* W177 (M282): Persistent ticking or rattling with no diagnosis
* Any generation: Poor service history — these engines are reliable but need good maintenance
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The A200 Petrol badge spans two very different engines. The W176's M270 2.0-litre is a proven, reliable unit that's well-regarded by owners. The W177's M282 1.3-litre is a modern, efficient engine co-developed with Renault-Nissan, but it has known issues: oil leaks, ticking noises, and carbon buildup. Both are good engines when properly maintained, but the M270 has the edge on long-term reliability. Buy on documented history, not on faith. A well-maintained A200 is a £15,000–£30,000 premium hatchback; a neglected one is a £5,000 engine replacement waiting to happen.

cta.label: → Compare A200 petrol engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes A200 Petrol (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for ticking, rattling, or hesitation (M282: timing chain or injector warning)
* Service History — including which engine era (M270 or M282) and confirmed oil-change intervals using MB-spec oil
* Oil Leak Check (M282) — Check for any evidence of oil leaks around the turbocharger area (recall issue)
* Turbo Recall (M282) — Has the turbocharger oil feed line recall been completed?
* Smoke Check — Blue smoke on startup can indicate turbo or valve seal issues
* Carbon Buildup Check (M282) — Any history of rough idle, hesitation, or loss of power? (intake valve carbon warning)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved timing, fuel, or turbo issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

items.label: * M270DE20LA (W176)
items.value: Displacement: 1,991 cc · Cylinders: 4-cylinder inline, 4 valves per cylinder · Compression Ratio: 9.8:1 · Fuel System: Direct injection · Turbocharger: Single twin-scroll turbocharger · Valvetrain: DOHC with variable valve timing

items.label: * M282DE14AL (W177)
items.value: Displacement: 1,332 cc · Cylinders: 4-cylinder inline, 4 valves per cylinder · Compression Ratio: 10.6:1 · Fuel System: Direct injection · Turbocharger: Single turbocharger · Valvetrain: DOHC with variable valve timing · Chain-driven timing (not a belt)
